title: Disable podmans firewall entirely
body:
### Feature request description

I am using podman and nftables.
My firewall is a "default deny firewall" which means, that my base chain (one for each hook) blocks everything I do not allow explicitly.
This also blocks podmans network access (or rather the access of the pods).

### Suggest potential solution

Two solutions exist:
- Do not use multiple base chains (per hook). That means, that podman has to add its rules to my chains.
- Do all firewall stuff manually. Podman should only create the network, assign ips (static ips) and I take care of doing all the firewall stuff (forward, nat) myself. Podman should have a switch to turn off its automatic management of the firewall for this to work best.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

1. Keep the firewall of podman active if it can not be disabled.
2. Create the rules manually with a higher priority, so podman does not overwrite them.

Podman would still create rules I do not want to have.
These rules could still be "harmful". E.g. allow stuff I do not want to allow.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
> Do all firewall stuff manually. Podman should only create the network, assign ips (static ips) and I take care of doing all the firewall stuff (forward, nat) myself. Podman should have a switch to turn off its automatic management of the firewall for this to work best.

That is already supported. You just need to set the firewall_driver to none in containers.conf https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/common/docs/containers.conf.5.md

I did not find this solution in your official documentation:
https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman.1.html
It does not mention the firewall setting anywhere.

Thank you for your help!
You helped me a lot!
